question: Did Murthy’s loneliness advisory come before or after Nobel’s AP definition of loneliness?
answer:
Murthy’s loneliness advisory came **before** Nobel’s AP definition of loneliness. In the AP Valentine’s Day story, Murthy is described as having issued a U.S. surgeon general **public health advisory “last spring”** declaring loneliness and isolation an “epidemic” [Data: Sources (5084, 5085)].  

In that same story, Jeremy Nobel is later quoted defining loneliness as subjective—“the gap between the social connections you want to have and the ones you do have” [Data: Sources (5094)]. Since Nobel’s definition appears in the later AP piece (dated Feb. 10, 2024) that references Murthy’s earlier advisory, the advisory preceded Nobel’s AP definition [Data: Sources (5084, 5094)].

question: Did the Air Force’s unsafe PCB finding come before its Minuteman III cancer review?
answer:
Yes. The Air Force’s finding of unsafe PCB levels in Minuteman III launch control capsules was disclosed as part of the environmental sampling effort that accompanied (and fed into) the broader missile-community cancer review—so it came before the Air Force released its first cancer-review results. [Data: Sources (8280, 8281, 8279, 8278, +more)]

In early December 2023, the Air Force briefed reporters that none of the more than 2,000 air, water, and soil samples at the Montana and Wyoming missile bases showed harmful contamination, but **four locations inside the underground launch control capsules had unsafe PCB levels**. At the same time, the Air Force said it was **expanding its medical-records review** and that initial cancer-number findings would not be made public for “a month or so.” [Data: Sources (8280, 8281, 8282, 8278, +more)]

The Air Force then publicly reported its **first cancer-review data** in March 2024 (noting the data was still incomplete). [Data: Sources (3371, 3372, 3373, 3374, +more)]

question: Which return-to-work date came first: England’s NHS junior doctors or South Korea’s striking interns?
answer:
England’s NHS junior doctors had the earlier return-to-work date: their strike was due to end at **7 a.m. on Jan. 9** (after a six-day walkout) [Data: Sources (6554, 6493, 6549, +more)].

South Korea’s striking medical interns and residents were ordered to return by **Feb. 29** (a government-set deadline to avoid penalties) [Data: Sources (5968, 6088, 6416, 2517, 2573, +more)].

question: What happened first: Buckingham Palace’s cancer statement or Kate’s Windsor video message?
answer:
Buckingham Palace’s cancer statement happened first: Buckingham Palace announced King Charles III’s cancer diagnosis on **Feb. 5, 2024** [Data: Sources (4723, 4724, 4824, 4796, 5102, +more)].

Kate’s Windsor video message came later: her video announcement (recorded in Windsor and broadcast on **March 22, 2024**) disclosed that she had cancer and was undergoing chemotherapy [Data: Sources (3987, 3988, 4050, 4112, 1311, +more)].

question: Which happened first: the 11th Circuit’s 59-page opinion or the 3rd Circuit’s mask-refusal ruling?
answer:
The **11th Circuit’s 59-page opinion happened first**.

In the Florida editorial roundup describing the 11th Circuit matter, the 59-page opinion is identified as having been issued on a **Wednesday** (with the roundup dated Jan. 15, 2024), placing it in **mid-January 2024** [Data: Sources (7163, 7164, 7167, +more)].

By contrast, the **3rd Circuit’s mask-refusal ruling** (holding that refusing to wear a mask during a COVID-19 public health emergency is not protected First Amendment speech) is described as having been issued **Monday**, and the AP story carrying it is timestamped **Feb. 6, 2024** [Data: Sources (4756, 4757, 4758, 4759, 4760, +more)].

question: How did the proposed 2,000-seat increase compare with South Korea’s current 3,058 medical school admissions cap?
answer:
South Korea’s proposed increase of **2,000 medical school admission seats** would be added on top of the **current cap of 3,058** admissions per year. That means the cap would rise to **5,058** if implemented in full [Data: Sources (3975, 3938, 5400, 1370, 6420, +more)].

In proportional terms, adding 2,000 seats is **about a two-thirds increase** over the current 3,058 cap (2,000 ÷ 3,058 ≈ 65%), which multiple reports described as “about two-thirds” or “about 65%” [Data: Sources (1370, 6420, 5966, 2481, 3975, +more)].

question: What medical center was the site of both Lloyd Austin’s prostatectomy and his hospitalization?
answer: Lloyd Austin’s prostatectomy and his subsequent hospitalization both took place at **Walter Reed National Military Medical Center** [Data: Sources (6810, 7190, 5096, 4550, 5974, +more)].

question: Did Trump’s three Supreme Court nominations come before or after Roe v. Wade was overturned?
answer:
Trump’s three Supreme Court nominations came **before** Roe v. Wade was overturned. Multiple reports note that Trump “nominated three conservative justices” during his term and that those appointments “pav[ed] the way” for the Supreme Court’s later decision to overturn Roe [Data: Sources (7571, 7446, 3471, 7627, 5847, +more)].

Roe v. Wade was overturned **afterward**, in the Supreme Court’s **Dobbs v. Jackson Women’s Health Organization** decision on **June 24, 2022** [Data: Sources (400, 7364, 2894, 8492, 8968, +more)].

question: How did the bill’s minimum funding guarantee for tribes differ before and after 2031?
answer:
Before 2031, the bill set a **minimum funding guarantee** for tribes: each year tribes would receive **the greater of $7.75 million or 20% of the funds deposited into the opioid settlement account in the prior fiscal year**. [Data: Sources (7231, 5424, 5423, 2429, 2430, +more)]

Starting in 2031, an amendment removed the **minimum required dollar amount**. After that point, the state would provide **20% of the money deposited into the opioid settlement account during the prior fiscal year**, and **if deposits fall low enough, no funding would be required to go to tribes**. [Data: Sources (2430)]

question: How did the Maine House and France’s parliament differ in vote tallies on abortion-rights amendments?
answer:
The Maine House vote on the abortion-rights constitutional amendment was close and fell short of the supermajority needed: the House approved it **76–68** in an initial vote, which was not enough to reach the required two-thirds threshold to send it to voters [Data: Sources (531, +more)]. (A separate report described a later House vote on the same effort as **75–65**, also short of two-thirds) [Data: Sources (656, +more)].

In contrast, France’s parliament backed its abortion-rights constitutional change by overwhelming margins. France’s National Assembly approved the bill **493–30** [Data: Sources (8012, 8013, +more)], and the final approval in a special joint session of parliament at Versailles passed **780–72** [Data: Sources (2527, 2528, 3062, +more)].

question: Did Biden’s Walter Reed physical happen before or after his March 7, 2024 State of the Union?
answer:
President Biden’s annual physical at Walter Reed happened **before** his March 7, 2024 State of the Union address. The Associated Press reported that Biden’s doctor released a health memo on **Feb. 28, 2024**, following a physical that took Biden to Walter Reed National Military Medical Center for more than 2 1/2 hours. [Data: Sources (6185, 6186, +more)]

The Feb. 28 reporting also explicitly referenced “next week’s State of the Union address,” indicating the physical occurred in advance of the March 7 speech. [Data: Sources (6192, +more)]

question: From where did the pope whose Indonesia trip was postponed follow the Good Friday Via Crucis at the Colosseum?
answer: Pope Francis followed the Good Friday *Via Crucis* at the Colosseum **from the Casa Santa Marta (his Vatican residence)**, according to the Vatican press office statement. [Data: Sources (4472, 4471, +more)]

question: How long after the Feb. 3, 2023 derailment did Norfolk Southern agree to the $600 million settlement?
answer:
Norfolk Southern agreed to the $600 million class-action settlement a little more than a year after the Feb. 3, 2023 derailment (the settlement was announced on April 9, 2024) [Data: Sources (509, 510, 511, 513, 715, +more)].

That’s roughly **14 months** after the derailment [Data: Sources (509, 510, 511, 513, 715, +more)].

question: What invitation did both President Joe Biden and Jill Biden extend to Kate Cox?
answer:
President Joe Biden and first lady Jill Biden invited Kate Cox to attend the State of the Union address at the U.S. Capitol (set for March 7). [Data: Sources (7722, 7723, +more)]

The White House said Cox would sit with the first lady, and Cox accepted the invitation. [Data: Sources (7723, 2794, +more)]

question: Did the FDA–CBP e-cigarette seizure happen before or after the mifepristone case arguments?
answer:
The FDA–CBP e-cigarette seizure happened **before** the mifepristone case arguments. The seizure operation was reported as having been **intercepted in July** (and publicly announced in mid-December 2023) [Data: Sources (9021, 9024, 10034, 9191, 9222, +more)].

By contrast, the Supreme Court **heard arguments** in the mifepristone case on a **Tuesday in late March 2024** [Data: Sources (4251, 4252, 4258, 4281, 4292, +more)].

question: According to the agency led by Xavier Becerra, what law required Medicare-funded ERs to screen and stabilize patients?
answer:
The U.S. Department of Health and Human Services (HHS) led by Secretary Xavier Becerra pointed to the **Emergency Medical Treatment and Active Labor Act (EMTALA)** as the federal law that required emergency rooms that accept Medicare funding to **screen patients and provide stabilizing treatment** for emergency medical conditions. [Data: Sources (1634, 1635, 1375, 1983, 6461, +more)]

EMTALA applies broadly to **nearly all emergency rooms** because it covers hospitals that accept Medicare funds, and it requires them to provide a medical exam and stabilize patients before discharge or transfer. [Data: Sources (1635, 1375, 1647, 1824, 6609, +more)]

question: How did Ohio’s H.B. 68 override vote compare with Kansas’s SB 233 override vote?
answer:
Ohio’s H.B. 68 override succeeded by comfortable margins, while Kansas’s SB 233 override ultimately failed because supporters fell short in the House.

In Ohio, the House voted **65-28** to override Gov. Mike DeWine’s veto, and the Senate later overrode as well, meaning the veto override effort **passed** and the bill was enacted [Data: Sources (6874, 4294, 4871, +more)].

In Kansas, the Senate voted **27-13** to override Gov. Laura Kelly’s veto—**the exact two‑thirds margin needed**—but the House vote to override was **82-43**, which was **two votes short** of the required two‑thirds, so the override effort **failed and the bill died** [Data: Sources (2258, 2257, 2259, +more)].

question: Did Biden’s temporary-pier mission come before or after the IPC’s famine-warning window began?
answer:
Biden’s temporary-pier mission came **before** the IPC’s famine-warning window began.

In his March 7, 2024 State of the Union address, President Biden said he was directing the U.S. military to lead “an emergency mission to establish a temporary pier” off Gaza to increase humanitarian aid deliveries [Data: Sources (3048, 2986, 3049, 3050, 3047, +more)].

The IPC warning window referenced in the provided material starts **mid-March**: an IPC report warned that famine “could occur in Gaza any time between mid-March and May” without an end to hostilities and immediate access to essential supplies and services [Data: Sources (3721, 3720)].

question: What Type A virus did both the Texas patient and the interstate cattle testing requirement involve?
answer: Both the Texas patient’s infection and the new interstate cattle testing requirement involved **Type A H5N1 (avian influenza/bird flu)** [Data: Sources (16, 1830, 4209, 1849, 842, +more)].

question: How did Texas regulate minors’ access to gender-affirming care versus birth control?
answer:
Texas regulated the two areas very differently: it **banned** most forms of gender-affirming medical care for transgender minors, while it **required parental permission** for minors to obtain birth control (at least in the context of services funded through the federal Title X program). [Data: Sources (9653, 9654, 6399, 3283, 3284, +more)]

On **gender-affirming care**, Texas enacted a law (often referenced as **SB 14**) that prevents transgender minors from accessing **hormone therapies, puberty blockers, and transition surgeries**. The reporting also notes that minors who had already started medications that the law bans are required to be **weaned off in a “medically appropriate” manner**. [Data: Sources (9653, 9654, 6399, +more)] In addition to the ban itself, Texas officials—particularly the attorney general’s office—pursued investigations and information requests related to minors’ gender-affirming care, including seeking records from hospitals and advocacy groups. [Data: Sources (9652, 9653, 2348, 6397, 1487, +more)]

On **birth control**, Texas law requires that **minors have parental permission to get contraception**, and a federal appeals court (the 5th Circuit) ruled that this parental-consent requirement **does not conflict with Title X**, the federally funded family planning program. The court reasoned that Title X’s goal of encouraging family participation is not undermined by Texas’s goal of empowering parents to consent to their teen’s receiving contraceptives, and said the two “reinforce each other.” [Data: Sources (3283, 3284)]

question: Which AP investigation was published first: the PPE stockpile report or the shrimp supply report?
answer:
The **PPE stockpile** AP investigation was published first. It was first created/published on **Dec. 20, 2023** (“States are trashing troves of masks and pandemic gear as huge, costly stockpiles linger and expire”) [Data: Sources (9382, 9381, 9380, 9412, 9413, +more)].

The **shrimp supply** AP investigation (“AP finds grueling conditions in Indian shrimp industry that report calls ‘dangerous and abusive’”) was first published on **March 20, 2024** [Data: Sources (3882, 3852, 3881, 3853, 3854, +more)].

question: What happened first: the White House learning of Austin’s hospitalization or his public cancer disclosure?
answer:
The White House learning of Austin’s hospitalization happened first. Pentagon and White House accounts say the White House (via national security adviser Jake Sullivan) was notified on **Jan. 4** that Austin had been hospitalized since Jan. 1 [Data: Sources (7191, 6789, 6666, 6813, 5975, +more)].

Austin’s public cancer disclosure came later. The Pentagon publicly revealed on **Jan. 9** that Austin had prostate cancer and that his Dec. 22 procedure was cancer surgery; the White House also said that day that Biden had not been informed of the cancer diagnosis until then [Data: Sources (6780, 6817, 6781, 6852, 7191, +more)].

question: How did prosecutors and the Trumbull County grand jury differ on charging Brittany Watts?
answer:
Prosecutors in Trumbull County took the position that they had to keep the felony “abuse of a corpse” allegation against Brittany Watts moving through the system by presenting it to the grand jury, emphasizing that it was the grand jury’s job—not the prosecutor’s—to decide whether she should be indicted. Prosecutor Dennis Watkins said his office was “duty bound to follow Ohio law” and “obligated to present” the charge to the grand jury, framing the grand jury as the decision-maker on whether an indictment should issue. [Data: Sources (9423, 9424, 9148, +more)]

The Trumbull County grand jury, however, declined to return an indictment—i.e., it “no-billed” the case—meaning Watts would not be charged going forward on the abuse-of-a-corpse count. The prosecutor’s office announced that grand jurors chose not to indict her, ending the case at that stage. [Data: Sources (7020, 7022, +more)]

question: Did Trump authorize the CIA’s China social-media campaign before or after the July 2017 ACA repeal effort?
answer:
Trump authorized the CIA’s clandestine China social-media campaign **after** the July 2017 Affordable Care Act (ACA) repeal effort.

A Reuters report cited in the provided material says the CIA was authorized by then-President Donald Trump **in 2019** to launch a covert campaign on Chinese social media aimed at turning public opinion in China against the Chinese Communist Party [Data: Sources (3638, 3637)]. By contrast, the ACA repeal effort that Trump referenced as being blocked by Sen. John McCain occurred in **July 2017** [Data: Sources (10298, 9121)].

So, based on these dates, the CIA authorization came roughly **two years after** the July 2017 ACA repeal push [Data: Sources (3638, 10298, 9121)].

question: How did Arizona’s 1864 abortion ban differ from North Carolina’s 12-week abortion ban?
answer:
Arizona’s 1864 abortion ban was a **near-total ban**: it criminalized abortion at **all stages of pregnancy**, allowing it only when it was **necessary to save the pregnant patient’s life**, and it provided **no exceptions for rape or incest** [Data: Sources (598, 500, 8786, 807, 1276, +more)]. The law also carried **criminal penalties (two to five years in prison)** and was written to require prosecution of anyone who “provides, supplies or administers” drugs or instruments to cause a miscarriage, with the life-of-the-mother exception [Data: Sources (601, 600, 1845, 504, 1276, +more)].

By contrast, North Carolina’s law is a **12-week ban**—meaning abortion remains legal **up to 12 weeks of pregnancy** in most cases, rather than being prohibited from the start of pregnancy [Data: Sources (7512, 5108)]. In other words, Arizona’s 1864 law functioned as an across-the-board prohibition with only a life-saving exception, while North Carolina’s law set a gestational cutoff (12 weeks) that still permits abortions earlier in pregnancy [Data: Sources (598, 807, 5108, 7512)].

question: What monthly activity-hour requirement did both Georgia Pathways descriptions set for eligibility?
answer:
Both Georgia Pathways descriptions set an eligibility requirement of **80 hours per month** of qualifying activity (such as work, study, rehabilitation, or volunteering) [Data: Sources (4625, 4626, 5568, 6558, 10320, +more)].

In other words, applicants must **document 80 monthly hours** of approved activities to be eligible for coverage under Georgia’s Pathways program [Data: Sources (4625, 5568, 6558, 10320, 3945, +more)].

question: What scan did the Vatican say ruled out pneumonia for the pope who approved “Infinite Dignity”?
answer: The Vatican said a **CT scan** ruled out pneumonia for Pope Francis (the pope who approved the “Infinite Dignity” declaration) [Data: Sources (10307, 10306, 10365, 10399, 2697, +more)].

question: How did the EPA’s PM2.5 standard differ from its PFOA/PFOS drinking-water limit?
answer:
The EPA’s **PM2.5 (soot) standard** was an **ambient air-quality target**: it lowered the **annual** allowable concentration of fine particulate matter in outdoor air to **9 micrograms per cubic meter**, down from **12**, and it is a level that **states, counties and tribal governments must achieve over time** through planning and follow-on controls on sources like vehicles, power plants, industrial sites and (in some cases) wildfire smoke [Data: Sources (4922, 4924, 4927, +more)].

By contrast, the EPA’s **PFOA/PFOS drinking-water limit** was a **drinking-water contaminant limit** that set **extremely low numeric caps** for two specific PFAS chemicals—**PFOA and PFOS at 4 parts per trillion**—described as the **lowest level they can be reliably detected/measured**, with required **monitoring and treatment actions by water providers** if levels are too high [Data: Sources (537, 581, 579, 533, +more)].

question: Did Austin’s disclosure come before or after Emhoff’s Feb. 27, 2024 hunger announcement?
answer:
Austin’s disclosure came **before** Emhoff’s Feb. 27, 2024 hunger announcement.  

The AP timeline on Austin’s prostate cancer and hospitalization secrecy says the Defense Department disclosed Austin’s cancer diagnosis on **Jan. 9, 2024** (and Austin later addressed the matter publicly in a Pentagon press conference on **Feb. 1, 2024**) [Data: Sources (6817, 6780, 4547, +more)].  

Emhoff’s hunger announcement (the $1.7 billion in new commitments) is dated **Feb. 27, 2024** [Data: Sources (6056, 6058, +more)].

question: Why did the monarch diagnosed with an enlarged prostate on Jan. 17, 2024 publicly disclose his cancer diagnosis?
answer:
King Charles III publicly disclosed his cancer diagnosis for two main reasons Buckingham Palace itself highlighted: to help public understanding of cancer, and to head off speculation. The palace said he shared the diagnosis “in the hope it may assist public understanding for all those around the world who are affected by cancer” and also “to prevent speculation” in a fast-moving media environment [Data: Sources (4725, 4832, 4969, 4970, 4726, +more)].

The disclosure also fit a broader pattern of using royal health announcements as a public-health prompt. When Charles announced his earlier benign enlarged-prostate procedure (diagnosed Jan. 17, 2024), it was described as an effort to encourage other men to get checked in line with public health advice, and charities reported surges in interest after the royal announcements [Data: Sources (7853, 7953, 7429, 7431, 7342, +more)]. After the cancer announcement, Cancer Research UK reported a sharp rise in visits to its cancer information pages—evidence, advocates said, that high-profile disclosures can prompt people to seek information and think about their own health [Data: Sources (4969, 2662, 4730, 5102, 5642, +more)].

Finally, multiple reports framed the decision as a deliberate break from centuries of royal secrecy about illness—an adaptation to modern communications pressures and the realities of social media, where information vacuums can quickly fill with rumor and misinformation [Data: Sources (4964, 7427, 7425, 4729, 3151, +more)].

question: How did Dr. Vivek Murthy’s loneliness warning compare with Dr. Jeremy Nobel’s definition?
answer:
Dr. Vivek Murthy framed loneliness as a large-scale public health emergency, issuing an advisory that described loneliness and isolation as an “epidemic” with “dire consequences” [Data: Sources (5084, 5085, 5086, +more)]. He also emphasized severity and health impact, warning that widespread loneliness can pose health risks “as deadly as smoking up to 15 cigarettes a day,” and said it costs the health industry billions of dollars annually [Data: Sources (5085, +more)]. Murthy further characterized loneliness as a biologically meaningful signal—“like hunger or thirst,” a feeling the body sends when something needed for survival is missing [Data: Sources (5085, 5086, +more)].

Dr. Jeremy Nobel, by contrast, defined loneliness more as an individual, subjective experience: “the gap between the social connections you want to have and the ones you do have” [Data: Sources (5094, +more)]. He also noted that loneliness can take “many forms,” ranging from physical isolation to rejection based on difference [Data: Sources (5092, +more)]. While Nobel agreed on serious health stakes—saying evidence is “quite clear” that loneliness increases risk of early mortality—his definition centers on perceived connection rather than the population-level “epidemic” framing Murthy used [Data: Sources (5092, +more)].

question: Did the EPA soot rule come before or after the 5th Circuit’s March 12, 2024 Title X decision?
answer:
The EPA soot rule came **before** the 5th Circuit’s March 12, 2024 Title X decision.

The soot rule was **finalized Wednesday, Feb. 7, 2024** [Data: Sources (4920, 4922, 4924, 4927, 4928, +more)]. The 5th Circuit’s Title X decision was issued **Tuesday, March 12, 2024** [Data: Sources (3283, 3284)].

question: In what year did Philip Morris submit its Zyn marketing application to the agency that set the LDT approval deadline?
answer:
Philip Morris submitted its Zyn marketing application in **2020** to the U.S. Food and Drug Administration (FDA) [Data: Sources (1804, 6339, +more)].

The provided material does not specify the “LDT approval deadline” or identify the agency that set that deadline; it only states that FDA officials are reviewing the Zyn marketing application and that it has been pending since 2020 [Data: Sources (1804, 6339, +more)].

question: Did the FDA approve GLP-1 drugs before or after its January 2024 suicidality review?
answer:
The FDA’s January 2024 suicidality review came **after** the agency had already approved multiple GLP-1 receptor agonist drugs. In that January update, the FDA said it was analyzing reports related to “more than a dozen drugs approved since 2005” in the GLP-1 class and that its preliminary evaluation had not found evidence the medicines cause suicidal thoughts or actions (while it could not rule out a small risk) [Data: Sources (7069, 7070, 7071, +more)].

Specific GLP-1 approvals mentioned in the provided material also predate January 2024—for example, Wegovy was approved for obesity in June 2021, and Zepbound was approved for weight management in November 2023 [Data: Sources (6645, 6523, 6524, 8178, +more)].

question: How did the mifepristone case and Idaho emergency abortion case differ in their legal focus?
answer:
The **mifepristone case** was legally focused on **federal drug regulation and access to a medication abortion pill**—specifically, challenges to the **Food and Drug Administration’s decisions** approving mifepristone and later **easing conditions for obtaining it** (including changes made in 2016 and 2021 that expanded access such as mail distribution and reduced in-person requirements). A major legal issue discussed in that case was also **whether the challengers had “standing” (the legal right to sue)** to attack the FDA’s actions. [Data: Sources (4252, 4162, 4288, 4292, 4123, +more)]

By contrast, the **Idaho emergency abortion case** was legally focused on **federal preemption and emergency-care obligations**—i.e., whether Idaho’s near-total abortion ban (allowing abortion only to save the pregnant patient’s life) **conflicts with EMTALA**, a federal law requiring Medicare-participating hospitals to **stabilize patients with emergency medical conditions**, which the Biden administration argues can include abortion in certain emergencies. In other words, it is a dispute about **how a federal emergency-treatment statute interacts with (and potentially overrides) a state abortion ban in emergency rooms**, not about FDA drug approval. [Data: Sources (1647, 6609, 1759, 1634, 1946, +more)]

question: Which CDC publication came first: the Puerto Rico cesarean report or the overdose-smoking study?
answer:
The **Puerto Rico cesarean report came first**. The Puerto Rico story describes a CDC report that was “released Wednesday,” and the article record is timestamped **2024-01-10** [Data: Sources (6830, +more)].

The **overdose-smoking study came later**. The overdose route-of-use story describes a CDC study “published Thursday,” and the article record is timestamped **2024-02-15** [Data: Sources (5385, +more)].

question: What decision did both Iowa and Nebraska make about the 2024 Summer EBT program?
answer:
Both **Iowa and Nebraska decided not to participate (opt out) of the federal 2024 Summer EBT program** [Data: Sources (6768, 9955, 1056, 5473, +more)].

In reporting on the rollout of Summer EBT, both states were listed among those that **chose not to participate in summer 2024**, with officials citing that they already had other summer feeding programs in place as a reason for declining the new benefit [Data: Sources (6768, 9957, 5473, +more)].

question: On what date was the White House informed about the hospitalization at the hospital where Austin had a prostatectomy?
answer:
The White House was informed of Defense Secretary Lloyd Austin’s hospitalization at Walter Reed National Military Medical Center (where he had undergone a prostatectomy on Dec. 22) on **Jan. 4, 2024** [Data: Sources (6789, 7191, 6698, 6665, 6812, +more)].

Multiple accounts specify that neither the White House nor President Joe Biden was notified until **Thursday, Jan. 4**, after Austin had been hospitalized since Jan. 1 due to complications from the surgery [Data: Sources (6789, 6665, 6812, 6698, 6811, +more)].

question: Did Evers sign the dental-care package before or after urging PFAS funding release?
answer:
Gov. Tony Evers signed the bipartisan dental-care legislative package **after** he urged Republicans to release PFAS funding. Evers signed the dental-care package into law on **Wednesday, Jan. 31, 2024** [Data: Sources (8128, 8129)].

Evers’ urging to release PFAS funding occurred earlier, during his **State of the State address on Tuesday, Jan. 23, 2024**, when he renewed his call for Republicans to release **$125 million** approved in the state budget to fight PFAS pollution [Data: Sources (7617, 7625, 7727, 7735, +more)].

question: Which vote happened first: France’s Versailles session or the Maine House vote?
answer:
France’s **Versailles joint-session vote happened first**.

French lawmakers approved the constitutional amendment in a special joint session at the Palace of Versailles on **Monday, March 4, 2024** [Data: Sources (2527, 2528, 2530, +more)].

The Maine House vote referenced in the provided material occurred later, on **Tuesday, April 9, 2024** [Data: Sources (531, 656, +more)].

question: How did the format and purpose differ between Kate’s video message and Buckingham Palace’s statement?
answer:
Kate’s message was delivered as a **recorded video in her own voice**, creating a direct, personal address to the public. In it, she spoke candidly about the “huge shock” of the diagnosis, explained that cancer was found after her January abdominal surgery, and said she was in the early stages of “preventative chemotherapy.” She also framed the announcement around her family—saying they needed time to explain the situation to their children—and asked for “time, space and privacy” while she completes treatment. [Data: Sources (4051, 4052, 4053, 3988, 3992, +more)]

By contrast, Buckingham Palace’s response was a **formal written palace statement** issued on King Charles’ behalf. Rather than providing a first-person account or medical detail, it focused on the King’s reaction and the institution’s supportive posture—saying Charles was “so proud of Catherine for her courage in speaking as she did,” that he had remained in close contact, and that he and Queen Camilla “will continue to offer their love and support” to the family. [Data: Sources (3990, 3991)]

question: What date did the U.S. defense secretary hospitalized at Walter Reed publicly disclose his prostate cancer diagnosis?
answer: The U.S. defense secretary (Lloyd Austin) publicly disclosed his prostate cancer diagnosis on **Jan. 9, 2024**, when the Defense Department released a statement from his doctors revealing the diagnosis and that his Dec. 22 procedure was prostate surgery [Data: Sources (6817, 6780, 6852, 7191, 7034, +more)].

question: Did North Carolina expand Medicaid before or after H5N1 was detected in dairy cows?
answer:
North Carolina expanded Medicaid **before** H5N1 (bird flu) was detected in dairy cows. Medicaid expansion in North Carolina **began Dec. 1, 2023** [Data: Sources (9366, 9014, 6931, 0, 10460, +more)].

By contrast, avian influenza (Type A H5N1) was **first detected/confirmed in dairy cows in March 2024** [Data: Sources (1832, 1873, 4210, 1852, 1831, +more)].

question: If opponents won, to how many weeks would use of the drug whose FDA label listed ER visits be limited?
answer:
If abortion opponents won, the window for using **mifepristone** (the drug whose FDA label lists emergency-room visits) would be **shortened to seven weeks of pregnancy** (from the current 10 weeks) [Data: Sources (8921, 8967, 4155, 8913, 8920, +more)].

Related reporting notes that the FDA prescribing label mentions that **2.9% to 4.6%** of women taking mifepristone go to the emergency room, which was cited in court arguments about the drug’s safety and access rules [Data: Sources (4130, 4284, 4129, 4283, 4123, +more)].

question: What fever-free timeframe did the CDC require for both students and workers to return?
answer:
The CDC required that **both students and workers be fever-free for at least 24 hours** (i.e., “a day since they’ve had a fever”) before returning to school, work, or other regular activities, **without using fever-reducing medication** [Data: Sources (2338, 2341, 2357, 2345, 2497, +more)].

This “fever-free for 24 hours” threshold also appeared in the CDC’s earlier, pre-change isolation guidance for the general public (as a condition for ending isolation), again specifying **at least 24 hours fever-free without fever-reducing medicines** [Data: Sources (2345, 10455)].

question: How did Pope Francis address health concerns at Gemelli in 2023 versus Via Crucis in 2024?
answer:
In **2023 at Rome’s Gemelli hospital**, Francis addressed health concerns in a more *clinical, treatment-focused* way: the Vatican said he was hospitalized for bronchitis after having trouble breathing and that he was **discharged after receiving intravenous antibiotics** [Data: Sources (10366, 10259, 5918, 4142, +more)]. In other words, the response to the health issue centered on medical care and recovery following hospitalization.

By contrast, during the **Via Crucis (Way of the Cross) at the Colosseum in 2024**, Francis addressed health concerns in a more *pastoral and scheduling* way: the Vatican said he **skipped the Good Friday procession to “conserve his health”** and instead **followed the Via Crucis from Casa Santa Marta** (his Vatican residence) in order to preserve his strength for the Easter Vigil and Easter Sunday Mass [Data: Sources (4472, 4471, 4509, 4508, +more)]. The decision was described as last-minute, underscoring that the immediate response was to reduce exertion rather than seek public-facing participation [Data: Sources (4473, 4474, 4471, 4472, +more)].

question: Which came first: RECA’s scheduled expiration or Ohio’s Aug. 7, 2024 ballot certification deadline?
answer:
RECA’s scheduled expiration came first. The Radiation Exposure Compensation Act (RECA) “expires June 7” [Data: Sources (304)].

Ohio’s ballot certification deadline is later: Ohio law requires certification of the ballot 90 days before the Nov. 5, 2024 election, which is “Aug. 7 this year” [Data: Sources (1448, 1449, +more)].

question: What kind of Senate action did both John McCain and Cindy Hyde-Smith take?
answer:
Both John McCain and Cindy Hyde-Smith took the same kind of Senate action: they **objected** (i.e., raised an objection on the Senate floor to block or prevent a requested action from moving forward). [Data: Sources (6223, +more)]

In Hyde-Smith’s case, she **objected to a request for a vote** on Sen. Tammy Duckworth’s bill that would establish a federal right to in vitro fertilization (IVF) treatments, which effectively blocked the Senate from voting on it at that time. [Data: Sources (6223, +more)]

question: What did the EPA say about asbestos use at Savannah River Site versus U.S. chlor-alkali plants?
answer:
The EPA said its final rule **allows asbestos-containing sheet gaskets to continue being used until 2037 at the U.S. Department of Energy’s Savannah River Site in South Carolina**, specifically “to ensure that safe disposal of nuclear materials can continue on schedule” [Data: Sources (3653, 3652)].

By contrast, for the **U.S. chlor-alkali industry**, the EPA said **only eight chlor-alkali plants in the U.S. still use asbestos diaphragms** to produce chlorine and sodium hydroxide, and that these plants are **mostly located in Louisiana and Texas** [Data: Sources (3651, 3650)]. The EPA also said **asbestos-diaphragm use has been declining and now accounts for less than one-third of U.S. chlor-alkali production** [Data: Sources (3651, 3650)].

In terms of timing, the EPA said the rule **will ban imports of asbestos for chlor-alkali as soon as the rule is published**, while **prohibitions on chlor-alkali use will be phased in over five or more years** to provide what the agency called “a reasonable transition period” [Data: Sources (3651)].

question: By what date did President Yoon Suk Yeol’s government order striking interns and residents to return to work?
answer:
President Yoon Suk Yeol’s government ordered striking medical interns and residents to return to work **by Feb. 29, 2024** [Data: Sources (5968, 6417, 2516, 2573, 2702, +more)].  

Government officials repeatedly framed **Feb. 29** as the end-of-month deadline for returning to their hospitals, warning that those who missed it would face steps such as license suspensions and possible prosecution [Data: Sources (5967, 6415, 2517, 3971, 2455, +more)].
